title: Design which works with Vivado fails with Yosys+Vivado
body:
## Steps to reproduce the issue

```shell
git clone https://github.com/mithro/litex-buildenv-arty-yosys-xilinx
# Run with Yosys + Vivado
./build_top.sh
# Run with just Vivado
./build_top-v.sh
```

This should produce both a `top.bit` and `top.bin` which can be programmed with whatever method you like. Using openocd it would be something like;
```shell
openocd -f board/digilent_arty.cfg -c "init; pld load 0 top.bit; exit"
```

Connect using your favorite terminal emulator to the correct serial port. With Vivado, the system should boot up and show the LiteX BIOS. With Yosys nothing seems to happen.

The design as an lm32 CPU and an external DDR controller. It should boot a BIOS, train the memory and then wait for firmware to be loaded from either SPI flash or via the USB serial port.

If you compare to `min` branch which has a CPU and no DDR controller. It should work both with Vivado and Yosys.

It is unclear what version of Yosys I was using for this. I will try and reproduce with the latest Yosys and report back.
code_blocks: openocd -f board/digilent_arty.cfg -c "init; pld load 0 top.bit; exit"
